'''Zelosians''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] plants native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zelos II]].

==Biology and appearance==
Outwardly, Zelosians were nearly identical to [[Human/Legends|Humans]] in height, build, hair and skin pigmentation, and their ability to grow facial hair.<ref name=":0">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]''</ref> Because of this, Zelosians could keep their origins a closely guarded secret and were commonly misidentified as [[Near-Human]]s by outsiders. While their resemblance to humans had been suggested by some as a spectacular form of naturally evolved mimicry, many Zelosian biologists believed that their species was the product of genetic engineering, though there was no direct evidence to support this.<ref name=":1">{{SW|url=news/welcome-to-the-star-wars-jungle|text=Welcome to the ''Star Wars'' Jungle|int=Welcome to the Star Wars Jungle|nolive=1}}</ref> Thus, unlike many true Near-Humans, Zelosians were not known to be able to interbreed with any other humanoid race as it was quite hard, but not impossible, to conceive.<ref name=":2">[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']]</ref>

Because of their unusual ancestry, Zelosians possessed a number of plant-like characteristics. Their [[Blood/Legends|blood]] was a green<ref name=":2" /> [[Chlorophyll/Legends|chlorophyllic]] sap.<ref name=":0" /> Additionally, a large portion of the muscles of Zelosians were cellulose fibers, with some form of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nastic_movements nastic response] apparently being involved in their reflexes.<ref name=":2" /> Zelosians still consumed vegetable and animal product-based foods, but could also photosynthesize through their skin.<ref name=":0" /> [[Celot Ratua Dil]] believed that he could survive one to two months on only sunlight and water.<ref name=":2" />

All Zelosians possessed emerald-green eyes. They were also night-blind, being unable to see whatsoever in light dimmer than that provided by a full moon. This meant that they stayed indoors after sunset and held many superstitions about the night (although their suspicions about [[Zombie/Legends|zombies]] proved to be somewhat true, being actually caused by corpses reanimated by [[Parasite/Legends|parasitic]] [[Worm/Legends|worms]] known as [[jumper]]s).<ref name=":0" />

[[Alcohol/Legends|Alcohol]] had no intoxicating effect on Zelosians. However, refined [[Sugar/Legends|sugar]] had a comparable one; a tablespoon would have affected Zelosians as four ounces of strong liquor might affect a Human.<ref name=":0" />

==Culture and society==
Zelosian culture was centered around the dichotomy between day and night. Dark was bad, while light was synonymous with good. They were a very superstitious species, with numerous beliefs regarding luck.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
Zelos II was subjugated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] for ore [[Mining/Legends|mining]], but the Zelosians were kept ignorant that the mining work they did supported the Imperial war machine. After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the Zelosians believed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] military had abandoned Zelos II, not knowing of a hidden base in the [[Valley of Umbra]]; the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] sent in forces to take out the base, but walked into an ambush. In the wake of the seeming Imperial abandonment of the planet, Zelosian [[Galleros Nul]] declared martial law, making himself [[Mandated Commander of the People of Zelos]]. When the Imperial presence on Zelos II was exposed by [[Jai Raventhorn]], Nul disavowed the Empire entirely and petitioned entrance for his world to the New Republic.
